content: hi, I am currently finished writing and security abstracting the tty0tty module and I want to upstream it. Unfortunately, I am currently working in old rust branch, and I would like to know if rust branch still accepts new patches. Or if I want to upstream my work, I have to port the current code to rust-next branch.
See https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#rust, TLDR: you have to port your code to rust-next.
Note also that if you mean actually to merge into the kernel, there needs to be an in-tree usecase. Unfortunately can't be a rewrite of an existing driver
Not sure what you are working on but just FYI

What means "in-tree usecase"? Sorry, i am new to linux. The tty0tty driver is not in the mainline, i decide to work on it because this issue i found on RFL(https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/62)
Usually the abstractions to support a module don't get merged unless they have a driver that uses them
I'm not sure whether tty0tty would qualify here. But it does make a nice sample
Just post it as a PR first before sending it to the list, so we get a better idea of what you're doing
OK, i will first post a PR, could i post it to rust branch? i am not sure whether it can work on rust-next branch.
The rust branch hasn’t been up to date for a while, so it needs to go against rust-next. For what reason wouldn’t you be able to work on it?

I'm not sure, because I (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-l-8WrGHEGI), follow the instructions given by the built environment, can only be applied on an old rust branch of, in the new branch will rust - next mistake, can't generate img files, So I can't test whether my module will work in the rust-next environment in my current environment.

The process isn't that far off, you should be able to get what you have working. There are some more up to date docs here https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/next/rust/index.html, you should follow the quickstart and just get the latest kernel built with some rust samples. (Build the kernel from master or rust-next before you cherry pick your driver and try to get that to build, just to make sure things work).

I tried the things in quick-start and they worked, and I can also see that make LLVM=1 rustavailable works. But my main problem seems to be with busybox, because you know I need to run the kernel through qemu, but for the rust-next branch it seems impossible to generate the corresponding img file through busybox. someone told me it is because rust-next branch doesn't have qemu-busybox-min.configany instruction about it?
In my kernel branch I just copied those files, see https://github.com/ariel-miculas/linux/commit/3a24e0bef55fe8bf1aa8a9549630ce70900c36d7
ok, i will try to add those file and see if it can work
